title: classifyTool: toolType !== 'hand' is the only pass-through — selection is drawing mode
rationale: Wave 3b atlasdraw-dd91 option (a): isDrawingMode = toolType !== 'hand'. Selection tool now included in drawing set — geo-elements are clickable with selection. Hand is the sole map-pan tool. Prior decision mx-d75e98 had selection as pass-through because no geo-elements existed yet; Wave 3b's PinTool created elements that must be clickable. Trade-off: users must switch to hand tool explicitly to pan map (cannot click-drag while in selection mode). Codified in classifyTool.ts JSDoc; tools tests updated from 17→24 cases (+7 PinTool). Arrow/freedraw/polyline scaleMode:hybrid deferred to atlasdraw-375a.

title: classifyTool: only 'hand' is pass-through (supersedes selection-also-pass-through)
rationale: Wave 3a shipped classifyTool returning false for both 'hand' AND 'selection' per plan literal — meaning selection let pointerEvents fall through to MapLibre. Wave 3b prerequisite atlasdraw-dd91 surfaced the consequence: once PinTool ships, the user's first click on a placed pin (with selection tool active) would pan the map instead of selecting the pin. Phase 1 simplicity favored option (a): make selection drawing-mode (Excalidraw captures clicks) so geo-elements are selectable. Trade-off: hand tool (keyboard 'h' or hand button) is now the only way to pan the map; selection no longer doubles as map-pan. Phase 4 toolbar UX may revisit if dual-purpose selection is needed.
